g.text: EXPLAIN SELECT name, age FROM users WHERE age > 21
QUERY PLAN (planner output — written before bytecode)
int operand 1: cursor# or register# or value
int operand 2: jump target or register# or page#
int operand 3: register# or column# or count
Phase 2 — Index Seek + Table Fetch (addr 4-8, per match)
index cost = height(3) + leaf_pages + rows x 4.0
COMPARE: Eq Ne Lt Gt Le Ge IsNull
CONTROL: Init Goto Next IdxNext Halt

div.p: addr opcode p1 p2 p3 comment
0 Init 0 1 0 start: jump to addr 1 1 OpenRead 0 5 3 cursor 0 = users (root=pg5, 3 cols) 2 OpenIndexRead 1 8 0 cursor 1 = idx_users_age (root=pg8) 3 Integer 21 3 0 r3 = constant 21 (WHERE value)

g.text: TWO cursors = planner selected index scan path
OpenIndexRead: B+tree cursor (leaf nodes only have data)
Integer: load WHERE constant once before loop

div.p: addr opcode p1 p2 p3 comment

4 IdxSeek 1 12 3 seek index B+tree to age >= r3 5 IdxKey 1 4 0 r4 = current age value from index 6 Gt 4 11 3 if r4 > r3: pass; else jump -> 11 7 IdxRowid 1 5 0 r5 = rowid from (age, rowid) entry 8 SeekRowid 0 5 0 seek TABLE cursor 0 to rowid in r5

g.text: addr 4-6: scan index B+tree leaf chain forward
IdxRowid: extracts rowid stored in index leaf entry
SeekRowid: SECOND B-tree descent in table file
Cost: O(log n) random I/O per matching row
Covering index would eliminate addr 8 entirely

div.p: addr opcode p1 p2 p3 comment

9 Column 0 0 1 r1 = cursor0.col0 (name) 10 Column 0 1 2 r2 = cursor0.col1 (age) 11 ResultRow 1 2 0 emit r1..r2 to user callback 12 IdxNext 1 5 0 advance index leaf; loop -> addr 5 13 Close 1 0 0 close index cursor 1 14 Close 0 0 0 close table cursor 0 15 Halt 0 0 0 done (return code = 0)

g.text: IdxNext: follows leaf-chain link in index B+tree
Close: decrements pin_count, frees cursor memory
